text.title: bun-runtime Failure Modes
text.summary: Five Bun-runtime antipatterns observed across 2025-2026 migrations.

antipattern.reason: Reproducibility dies. New Bun releases routinely change defaults; CI green today, red tomorrow.

antipattern.description: Using `bun --hot` for dev expecting clean reloads.
antipattern.reason: --hot persists module-level state. Config changes do not take effect until a full restart; developers chase phantom bugs.

antipattern.description: Importing `node:http` and `node:https` for the HTTP layer on Bun.
antipattern.reason: Loses Bun.serve's JavaScriptCore optimisations; the runtime layer becomes a polyfill, not a feature.

antipattern.description: Using `fs.readFileSync` for every static asset read.
antipattern.reason: Bun.file is lazy and faster. Repeated synchronous reads block the event loop for no reason.
Lockfile drift goes undetected; dependent CI jobs install slightly different versions per run, producing flaky builds.
